AL.name: Alabama
AL.notes: Limited state-specific programs. Apply for hospital charity care directly.

AK.name: Alaska
AK.notes: Contact Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium if applicable. Apply for hospital charity care directly.

AZ.name: Arizona

programs.name: AHCCCS (Arizona Health Care Cost Containment System)
programs.description: Arizona's Medicaid program. Some international students on specific visas may qualify for emergency coverage.

AZ.notes: Emergency Medicaid may cover qualifying emergencies regardless of immigration status.

AR.name: Arkansas
AR.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

CA.name: California

programs.name: Medi-Cal
programs.description: California's Medicaid program. Some international students may qualify based on income. Emergency Medi-Cal covers emergency services for income-eligible patients regardless of immigration status.

programs.name: California Health Safety Net
programs.description: Many California hospitals participate in the Health Safety Net program for low-income patients.

CA.notes: California has among the strongest patient protections. SB 370 requires hospitals to screen patients for charity care before billing.

CO.name: Colorado

programs.name: Colorado Indigent Care Program (CICP)
programs.description: Discounted medical care for low-income Colorado residents regardless of immigration status.

CO.notes: CICP can provide significant discounts for qualifying patients.

CT.name: Connecticut

programs.name: HUSKY Health
programs.description: Connecticut's Medicaid program. Income-based eligibility.

CT.notes: Connecticut has strong hospital charity care requirements.

DC.name: District of Columbia

programs.name: DC Medicaid / Alliance
programs.description: DC Alliance provides primary care coverage to low-income DC residents regardless of immigration status.

DC.notes: DC Alliance is available to undocumented residents and some visa holders.

DE.name: Delaware
DE.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

FL.name: Florida
FL.notes: Florida has limited state programs for uninsured patients. Apply for hospital charity care directly. Florida hospitals are required to post their charity care policies.

GA.name: Georgia
GA.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly. Georgia has not expanded Medicaid.

HI.name: Hawaii

programs.name: Med-QUEST
programs.description: Hawaii's Medicaid program with broad eligibility.

HI.notes: Hawaii has broad healthcare coverage requirements.

ID.name: Idaho
ID.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

IL.name: Illinois

programs.name: Illinois Medicaid
programs.description: Illinois Medicaid covers emergency services. AllKids covers children.

IL.notes: Illinois has strong hospital financial assistance requirements under the Hospital Uninsured Patient Discount Act.

IN.name: Indiana

programs.name: HIP 2.0 (Healthy Indiana Plan)
programs.description: Income-based health coverage for adults.

IN.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

IA.name: Iowa
IA.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

KS.name: Kansas
KS.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

programs.name: Kentucky Medicaid
programs.description: Kentucky expanded Medicaid under the ACA.

LA.name: Louisiana
LA.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

ME.name: Maine
ME.notes: Maine expanded Medicaid under the ACA.

MD.name: Maryland

programs.name: Maryland Medicaid
programs.description: Maryland's Medicaid program.

MD.notes: Maryland has strong patient billing protections.

MA.name: Massachusetts

programs.name: Health Safety Net (HSN)
programs.description: Covers low-income Massachusetts residents who are uninsured or underinsured, including some non-citizens. Free or low-cost care at participating hospitals.

programs.name: MassHealth
programs.description: Massachusetts Medicaid program with broad eligibility, including some non-citizen visa holders.

MA.notes: Massachusetts has the strongest patient protections in the country. The Health Safety Net covers many international residents.

MI.name: Michigan
MI.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly. Michigan has expanded Medicaid.

programs.name: Minnesota Medicaid / MinnesotaCare
programs.description: MinnesotaCare provides coverage for low-income Minnesotans.

MS.name: Mississippi
MS.notes: Mississippi has not expanded Medicaid. Apply for hospital charity care directly.

MO.name: Missouri
MO.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

MT.name: Montana
MT.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

NE.name: Nebraska
NE.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

NV.name: Nevada
NV.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

NH.name: New Hampshire
NH.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

NJ.name: New Jersey

programs.name: NJ FamilyCare
programs.description: New Jersey's Medicaid program.

NJ.notes: New Jersey has strong hospital charity care requirements under the Charity Care law.

NM.name: New Mexico
NM.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

NY.name: New York

programs.name: Emergency Medicaid (NY)
programs.description: New York's Emergency Medicaid covers emergency medical services for income-eligible individuals regardless of immigration status, including undocumented residents.

programs.name: NY State of Health
programs.description: New York's health insurance marketplace.

NY.notes: New York has among the strongest protections for non-citizens. Emergency Medicaid is broadly available. Many NYC public hospitals (H+H system) have extremely robust charity care.

NC.name: North Carolina
NC.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

ND.name: North Dakota
ND.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

OH.name: Ohio
OH.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly. Ohio has expanded Medicaid.

OK.name: Oklahoma
OK.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

OR.name: Oregon

programs.name: Oregon Health Plan (OHP)
programs.description: Oregon's Medicaid program, expanded to cover more residents.

OR.notes: Oregon has a robust Medicaid program.

PA.name: Pennsylvania

programs.name: PA Medicaid (Medical Assistance)
programs.description: Pennsylvania's Medicaid program.

PA.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

RI.name: Rhode Island
RI.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

SC.name: South Carolina
SC.notes: South Carolina has not expanded Medicaid. Apply for hospital charity care directly.

SD.name: South Dakota
SD.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

TN.name: Tennessee
TN.notes: Tennessee has not expanded Medicaid. Apply for hospital charity care directly.

TX.name: Texas

programs.name: Texas Medicaid (CHIP)
programs.description: Children's Medicaid program. Adult eligibility is extremely limited in Texas.

TX.notes: Texas has very limited Medicaid for adults. However, Texas law (Sec. 311.031) requires nonprofit hospitals to provide charity care. This is your strongest path in Texas.

UT.name: Utah
UT.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

programs.name: Vermont Medicaid
programs.description: Vermont's Medicaid program with broad eligibility.

VA.name: Virginia
VA.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly. Virginia expanded Medicaid in 2019.

WA.name: Washington

programs.name: Apple Health (WA Medicaid)
programs.description: Washington State's Medicaid program, expanded to cover more residents including some visa holders.

WA.notes: Washington has broad Medicaid eligibility and strong patient protections.

WV.name: West Virginia
WV.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

WI.name: Wisconsin
WI.notes: Apply for hospital charity care directly.

WY.name: Wyoming
WY.notes: Wyoming has not expanded Medicaid. Apply for hospital charity care directly.
